additionalTime: 10 minutes poblano steam time plus 20 minutes flavor rest
author: Robert Carr
description: Roasted green salsa with tomatillos, poblano, jalapeno, serrano, garlic, onion, cilantro, lime, and a small amount of honey.
equipment: Bowl or foil for steaming poblano
ingredients: 1 serrano pepper, optional for extra heat
1/2 cup packed cilantro leaves and tender stems
1 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, or 1/2 teaspoon Morton kosher salt
instructions: Place 1 large poblano pepper in a dry cast-iron skillet or directly over a gas flame and char it for 6 to 8 minutes, turning often, until the skin is blistered and blackened in patches.
Transfer the poblano to a covered bowl or wrap it loosely in foil and let it steam for 10 minutes.

While the poblano steams, place 1 1/2 pounds tomatillos, 2 jalapenos, 1 serrano pepper if using, 1/2 small white onion, and 4 unpeeled garlic cloves in a dry cast-iron skillet or comal over medium-high heat.
Roast for 10 to 14 minutes, turning occasionally, until the tomatillos soften and turn olive-green, the peppers blister, the onion chars in spots, and the garlic is browned and soft inside.
Rub most of the blackened skin from the poblano, then remove the stem and seeds.

Add the roasted tomatillos, peeled poblano, jalapenos, serrano if using, onion, peeled garlic, 1/2 cup cilantro, 1 1/2 tablespoons fresh lime juice, 1 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and 1/2 teaspoon honey if using to a blender.
For a rustic salsa verde, pulse or blend on low speed for 10 to 20 seconds, stopping before it becomes fully smooth.
For a smooth taqueria-style salsa verde, blend on medium-high speed for 20 to 30 seconds.
Taste and adjust only if needed with a small pinch of kosher salt, a few drops of lime juice, or a few drops of honey if the tomatillos are very sharp.
Let the salsa rest for at least 20 minutes before serving.
notes: The poblano is the upgrade. It adds roasted green chile depth without making the salsa taste heavy or overly spicy.
Do not rinse the poblano after charring. Rinsing washes away roasted flavor.
Black pepper is intentionally omitted because it can muddy the clean tomatillo, chile, cilantro, and lime flavor.
If the salsa tastes too tart, use the optional honey before adding more salt. Tomatillos can vary a lot in sharpness.
If using a Vitamix 5200, low pulses around Variable 2 to 4 keep the salsa rustic, while Variable 6 or 7 makes it smoother.
For a mild salsa verde, skip the serrano and remove the jalapeno seeds before blending.
This salsa works especially well with breakfast burritos, pork, chicken, eggs, grilled vegetables, tacos, and chips.
title: Roasted Poblano Salsa Verde
